title: HTTP Servers in Go 1.26: Secure Production APIs
description: Go developers who can build robust clients and relational data systems and now need production-grade origin services and HTTP APIs. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate go 1.26.5 net/http service lifecycle, semantics, servemux, handlers, middleware, request and response boundaries, body limits, streaming, context, server protocols and timeouts, concurrency, postgresql-backed repositories, consistency, authentication, authorization, cors and csrf, webhooks, ssrf, reverse proxies, smuggling defense, tests, fuzzing, race detection, observability, health, openapi 3.2, compatibility, deployment, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-go decision models plus explicit compile, race, listener, tls, postgresql, proxy, load, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative production service projects and a performance-based certification exam through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Go, HTTP client, SQL, accessibility, security, context, testing, and evidence habits before adding one server boundary.

Browser Go is deterministic and isolated. It models request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup decisions but never opens a listener, contacts a network or database, executes host commands, reads host state, or handles real credentials.

Passing work requires exact request and route inputs, authority and resource limits, response and effect evidence, changed and failure cases, cleanup and recovery, and explicit full-toolchain transfer limits.

competencies.statement: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
misconceptions: More routes and higher request throughput necessarily mean the service creates more user value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.

competencies.statement: Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.
misconceptions: The origin server receives the exact bytes, address, scheme, and authority originally sent by every client.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.

competencies.statement: Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.
misconceptions: One Go server process handles requests sequentially unless handlers explicitly start goroutines.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.

competencies.statement: Validate configuration, bind the intended listener, initialize dependencies, expose liveness separately from readiness, and fail startup atomically.
misconceptions: Listening on a port proves the service and all required dependencies are ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate configuration, bind the intended listener, initialize dependencies, expose liveness separately from readiness, and fail startup atomically.

competencies.statement: Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.
misconceptions: A 200 health response is a complete operational ownership and recovery plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.

competencies.statement: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
misconceptions: A JSON object, database row, and HTTP resource are necessarily the same entity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.

competencies.statement: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method semantics independently and preserve them through handler behavior.
misconceptions: PUT and DELETE are safe because repeated requests are expected to have the same intended effect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method semantics independently and preserve them through handler behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose status, Location, validators, and response content for create, replace, partial update, asynchronous work, and deletion.
misconceptions: Every successful write should return 200 with the updated object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose status, Location, validators, and response content for create, replace, partial update, asynchronous work, and deletion.

competencies.statement: Use If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.
misconceptions: ETags are cache decorations and cannot protect a write against stale state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use If-Match, If-None-Match, dates, 304, 412, and 428 to prevent lost updates and unnecessary transfer.

competencies.statement: Test method semantics against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, and changed resource state.
misconceptions: One happy-path status assertion proves an endpoint obeys HTTP semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test method semantics against retries, duplicate delivery, stale validators, HEAD, OPTIONS, and changed resource state.

competencies.statement: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
misconceptions: ServeMux patterns can match methods or paths but not a host and method together.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.

competencies.statement: Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
misconceptions: The last registered overlapping route always wins, so registration order resolves ambiguity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.

competencies.statement: Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.
misconceptions: PathValue always returns the exact percent-encoded bytes present in RequestURI.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Request.PathValue while distinguishing segment unescaping, escaped slash data, raw query, cleaned paths, and invalid identifiers.

competencies.statement: Account for GET matching HEAD, method-specific 405 Allow responses, OPTIONS behavior, host routes, and Go 1.26 temporary slash redirects.
misconceptions: A GET pattern rejects HEAD and trailing-slash redirects always use 301.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Account for GET matching HEAD, method-specific 405 Allow responses, OPTIONS behavior, host routes, and Go 1.26 temporary slash redirects.

competencies.statement: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.
misconceptions: If every intended path has a handler, the route table is complete and non-conflicting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.

competencies.statement: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
misconceptions: A handler returns a response value after ServeHTTP completes, like an ordinary function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.

competencies.statement: Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
misconceptions: Middleware wrappers commute, so changing their order cannot change security or response behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.

competencies.statement: Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
misconceptions: Creating dependencies inside each handler improves isolation and connection reuse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.

competencies.statement: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, serialization, and external effects so core rules are testable without HTTP.
misconceptions: A layered architecture requires one package per technical noun and many pass-through interfaces.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, serialization, and external effects so core rules are testable without HTTP.

competencies.statement: Guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes.
misconceptions: Calling WriteHeader again replaces the earlier status when a downstream error occurs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes.

competencies.statement: Interpret Method, URL, Host, RequestURI, Header, Body, Trailer, RemoteAddr, TLS, Pattern, and Context using server-side semantics.
misconceptions: Request.URL.Host always contains the authority used by an incoming origin-form request.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret Method, URL, Host, RequestURI, Header, Body, Trailer, RemoteAddr, TLS, Pattern, and Context using server-side semantics.

competencies.statement: Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.
misconceptions: A syntactically valid Host header is trustworthy because net/http parsed it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.

competencies.statement: Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.
misconceptions: The leftmost X-Forwarded-For value always identifies the real client.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.

competencies.statement: Parse query values, duplicate keys, empty values, encoding, canonicalization, and sensitive parameters without rebuilding ambiguous URLs.
misconceptions: Calling URL.Query removes all ambiguity and preserves the original query byte-for-byte.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse query values, duplicate keys, empty values, encoding, canonicalization, and sensitive parameters without rebuilding ambiguous URLs.

competencies.statement: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.
misconceptions: Logging RequestURI is safe because authorization values appear only in headers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.

competencies.statement: Handle case-insensitive field names, repeated values, list syntax, hop-by-hop fields, size limits, and canonical map behavior without naïve splitting.
misconceptions: Header.Get exposes every field value exactly as received and is sufficient for every repeated field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle case-insensitive field names, repeated values, list syntax, hop-by-hop fields, size limits, and canonical map behavior without naïve splitting.

competencies.statement: Validate request Content-Type and parameters, select an acceptable response representation, and return 415 or 406 with a documented default.
misconceptions: A JSON decoder proves the request media type is JSON and Accept can always be ignored.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate request Content-Type and parameters, select an acceptable response representation, and return 415 or 406 with a documented default.

competencies.statement: Set Content-Type, Content-Length strategy, Location, validators, Cache-Control, Vary, security fields, and trailers before committing response headers.
misconceptions: Headers can be changed after Write or WriteHeader and will update the transmitted response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set Content-Type, Content-Length strategy, Location, validators, Cache-Control, Vary, security fields, and trailers before committing response headers.

competencies.statement: Generate stable strong or weak validators from the selected representation or resource version and apply comparison semantics correctly.
misconceptions: A database update timestamp is automatically a strong validator for every negotiated representation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate stable strong or weak validators from the selected representation or resource version and apply comparison semantics correctly.

competencies.statement: Design shared and private caching, freshness, revalidation, no-store, stale behavior, invalidation, and Vary without exposing user-specific data.
misconceptions: Cache-Control private prevents browsers from storing a response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design shared and private caching, freshness, revalidation, no-store, stale behavior, invalidation, and Vary without exposing user-specific data.

competencies.statement: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.
misconceptions: Rejecting a large Content-Length guarantees the handler never reads excessive body bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use MaxBytesReader and server limits to bound actual body reads independently of absent, false, compressed, or chunked Content-Length.

competencies.statement: Decode exactly one JSON value, reject trailing content and unknown fields when the contract is closed, and distinguish syntax, type, and size failures.
misconceptions: Decoder.Decode once proves the body contains exactly one valid contract object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decode exactly one JSON value, reject trailing content and unknown fields when the contract is closed, and distinguish syntax, type, and size failures.

competencies.statement: Separate transport shape validation from domain invariants, normalize only defined values, accumulate safe field errors, and preserve causality internally.
misconceptions: Database constraint errors are the clearest client-facing field validation format.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate transport shape validation from domain invariants, normalize only defined values, accumulate safe field errors, and preserve causality internally.

competencies.statement: Bound URL-encoded and multipart parsing, memory and disk use, file count, part headers, filenames, content, temporary cleanup, and storage authority.
misconceptions: ParseMultipartForm maxMemory caps the total request and prevents temporary disk use.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound URL-encoded and multipart parsing, memory and disk use, file count, part headers, filenames, content, temporary cleanup, and storage authority.

competencies.statement: Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.
misconceptions: Handlers must always defer Request.Body.Close and fully drain attacker-controlled bodies.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Understand server-owned body closure, early rejection, draining and connection reuse, trailers, compression expansion, and read-before-write compatibility.

competencies.statement: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.
misconceptions: Writing a JSON body before WriteHeader still allows the handler to send a later 422 status.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.

competencies.statement: Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
misconceptions: Problem detail should include stack traces and SQL errors so clients can debug the server.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.

competencies.statement: Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.
misconceptions: Every returned Go error should become status 500 because callers cannot safely distinguish it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.

competencies.statement: Use ResponseController capability errors, flush, deadlines, full duplex, trailers, disconnects, heartbeats, and bounded producers deliberately.
misconceptions: Type-asserting http.Flusher is portable through every middleware and protocol wrapper.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ResponseController capability errors, flush, deadlines, full duplex, trailers, disconnects, heartbeats, and bounded producers deliberately.

competencies.statement: Stop stream producers on context cancellation, bound queues and bytes, preserve partial-response evidence, and avoid goroutine or resource leaks.
misconceptions: Once headers are sent, a streaming handler can report any later failure with a new HTTP status.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stop stream producers on context cancellation, bound queues and bytes, preserve partial-response evidence, and avoid goroutine or resource leaks.

competencies.statement: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
misconceptions: Request.Context remains valid until all goroutines started by the handler decide to finish.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.

competencies.statement: Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
misconceptions: Giving each dependency the full request timeout keeps total latency within the request deadline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.

competencies.statement: Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
misconceptions: All context cancellation is a server error that should increment the 5xx failure rate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.

competencies.statement: Give every request goroutine a stop path, bounded result channel, single closer, retained error, and join or deliberate ownership transfer.
misconceptions: A buffered result channel automatically prevents goroutine leaks after request cancellation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Give every request goroutine a stop path, bounded result channel, single closer, retained error, and join or deliberate ownership transfer.

competencies.statement: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.
misconceptions: Deferred cleanup always completes even after the request deadline expires.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.

competencies.statement: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
misconceptions: ReadTimeout alone safely handles every streaming upload and slow-client scenario.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.

competencies.statement: Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
misconceptions: Go 1.26 net/http automatically serves HTTP/3 whenever HTTP/2 is enabled.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.

competencies.statement: Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.
misconceptions: StrictMaxConcurrentRequests limits concurrent streams accepted by an HTTP/2 Server.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set HTTP2Config stream, frame, compression-table, flow-control, ping, write-byte, and error-count boundaries without confusing transport and server fields.

competencies.statement: Configure TLS identity, versions, certificates, ALPN, rotation, proxy termination, redirects, and plaintext policy with end-to-end scheme evidence.
misconceptions: TLS termination at any proxy proves the origin may trust every forwarded scheme and client field.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure TLS identity, versions, certificates, ALPN, rotation, proxy termination, redirects, and plaintext policy with end-to-end scheme evidence.

competencies.statement: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.
misconceptions: Server.Shutdown waits for hijacked connections and guarantees every cleanup callback has finished.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Shutdown deadlines, readiness withdrawal, listener stop, in-flight drain, hijacked-connection handling, RegisterOnShutdown, Close fallback, and exit evidence.

competencies.statement: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
misconceptions: A map is safe in handlers when each request modifies a different key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.

competencies.statement: Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
misconceptions: Starting one goroutine per request is bounded because the server controls request concurrency automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.

competencies.statement: Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
misconceptions: Rate limiting solely by RemoteAddr gives fair per-user protection behind proxies and NAT.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.

competencies.statement: Reject before expensive work when queue, dependency, memory, deadline, or priority budgets are exhausted and preserve recovery capacity.
misconceptions: Accepting and timing out excess work is fairer and safer than early load shedding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reject before expensive work when queue, dependency, memory, deadline, or priority budgets are exhausted and preserve recovery capacity.

competencies.statement: Measure active, queued, rejected, canceled, completed, saturated, and recovered work without high-cardinality labels and rehearse changed-load recovery.
misconceptions: A low average latency proves the server has enough capacity during burst and tail conditions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure active, queued, rejected, canceled, completed, saturated, and recovered work without high-cardinality labels and rehearse changed-load recovery.

competencies.statement: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
misconceptions: sql.Open creates and verifies one dedicated database connection for each handler.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.

competencies.statement: Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
misconceptions: Escaping quote characters makes string-concatenated SQL equivalent to parameter binding.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.

competencies.statement: Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
misconceptions: A transaction makes every repeated SELECT observe one fixed snapshot under PostgreSQL defaults.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.

competencies.statement: Map no rows, uniqueness, foreign key, serialization, deadline, cancellation, unavailable, and unknown failures without exposing schema internals.
misconceptions: Every database error is either 404 or 500 and may be matched from its message text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map no rows, uniqueness, foreign key, serialization, deadline, cancellation, unavailable, and unknown failures without exposing schema internals.

competencies.statement: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.
misconceptions: If the client disconnects before the response, the database transaction necessarily rolled back.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.

competencies.statement: Define stable transport schemas, domain models, persistence records, optional and null semantics, field ownership, and evolution boundaries separately.
misconceptions: Reusing database structs as JSON responses keeps the API and storage safely synchronized.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define stable transport schemas, domain models, persistence records, optional and null semantics, field ownership, and evolution boundaries separately.

competencies.statement: Build opaque cursor pagination from a unique stable order, bound page size, preserve filters and authorization, and test inserts and deletes between pages.
misconceptions: OFFSET pagination cannot skip or duplicate results when rows are inserted concurrently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build opaque cursor pagination from a unique stable order, bound page size, preserve filters and authorization, and test inserts and deletes between pages.

competencies.statement: Scope and validate idempotency keys, bind request fingerprints, serialize first execution, persist terminal outcome, expire records, and reject mismatched replay.
misconceptions: An Idempotency-Key header alone makes any handler idempotent without storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Scope and validate idempotency keys, bind request fingerprints, serialize first execution, persist terminal outcome, expire records, and reject mismatched replay.

competencies.statement: Use version fields or validators with conditional updates, distinguish absence from conflict, and return current-state recovery information safely.
misconceptions: Last-write-wins is optimistic concurrency control because every update eventually succeeds.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use version fields or validators with conditional updates, distinguish absence from conflict, and return current-state recovery information safely.

competencies.statement: Model PATCH semantics, missing versus explicit zero or null, immutable and server-owned fields, property authorization, atomic validation, and response evidence.
misconceptions: Decoding PATCH into the full resource struct reliably distinguishes omitted fields from zero values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model PATCH semantics, missing versus explicit zero or null, immutable and server-owned fields, property authorization, atomic validation, and response evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
misconceptions: Possession of any syntactically valid token proves the current user identity and permissions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.

competencies.statement: Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
misconceptions: A fast general-purpose hash with a unique salt is ideal for password storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.

competencies.statement: Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
misconceptions: A signed cookie is encrypted and therefore safe for secrets and unrestricted personal data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.

competencies.statement: Pin allowed algorithms and keys; verify signature, type, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, subject, token use, key rotation, and clock policy before claims.
misconceptions: A decoded JWT with a future exp claim is authenticated even if issuer and audience are unchecked.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin allowed algorithms and keys; verify signature, type, issuer, audience, expiration, not-before, subject, token use, key rotation, and clock policy before claims.

competencies.statement: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.
misconceptions: OAuth access tokens are login sessions and an API key is equivalent to user authentication.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat bearer access tokens as scoped resource-server credentials under RFC 6750 and BCP 9700 without implementing an authorization server casually.

competencies.statement: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
misconceptions: A role string alone completely describes authorization for every resource instance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.

competencies.statement: Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
misconceptions: Unpredictable UUIDs make object-level authorization checks optional.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.

competencies.statement: Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
misconceptions: JSON tags and omitempty prevent clients from setting privileged fields.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.

competencies.statement: Enforce function-level policy at a shared application boundary while retaining resource-specific checks and testing every route alias.
misconceptions: Hiding admin links in the client prevents ordinary users from invoking admin handlers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce function-level policy at a shared application boundary while retaining resource-specific checks and testing every route alias.

competencies.statement: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 consistently, limit side channels, record privacy-safe decision evidence, and test cross-tenant changed cases.
misconceptions: Returning 404 for every denial removes all authorization side channels and audit needs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 consistently, limit side channels, record privacy-safe decision evidence, and test cross-tenant changed cases.

competencies.statement: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.
misconceptions: CORS blocks non-browser clients and therefore protects an API from unauthorized writes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish origin, site, same-origin policy, CORS read permission, preflight, credentials, and server authorization.

competencies.statement: Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.
misconceptions: Reflecting Origin is safe whenever Access-Control-Allow-Credentials is false.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate exact origins, methods, requested headers, credential mode, exposed headers, max age, Vary, null origin, and rejection behavior.

competencies.statement: Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
misconceptions: SameSite cookies alone prevent every cross-site request forgery and browser edge case.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.

competencies.statement: Select host-only scope, __Host or __Secure prefixes, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Partitioned, expiry, and rotation for the intended browser flow.
misconceptions: HttpOnly prevents a browser from sending the cookie in a cross-site request.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select host-only scope, __Host or __Secure prefixes, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Partitioned, expiry, and rotation for the intended browser flow.

competencies.statement: Set content-type protection, framing, referrer, transport, content security, permissions, and cache policy according to whether responses are API data or HTML.
misconceptions: Security headers are interchangeable boilerplate and stricter values cannot break required browser flows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set content-type protection, framing, referrer, transport, content security, permissions, and cache policy according to whether responses are API data or HTML.

competencies.statement: Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
misconceptions: Re-encoding decoded JSON produces the same bytes and is safe for webhook signature verification.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.

competencies.statement: Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.
misconceptions: A valid signature proves a webhook event is new and has not already been processed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.

competencies.statement: Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.
misconceptions: Returning 2xx before recording the event is safe because the provider can always retry.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.

competencies.statement: Treat server-side URLs, redirects, DNS results, IP ranges, schemes, ports, credentials, and response size as egress policy enforced on every hop.
misconceptions: Validating the input hostname once prevents redirects and DNS rebinding from reaching private services.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat server-side URLs, redirects, DNS results, IP ranges, schemes, ports, credentials, and response size as egress policy enforced on every hop.

competencies.statement: Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.
misconceptions: A generic requests-per-minute limit prevents scraping, scalping, spam, and workflow automation abuse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.

competencies.statement: Trace downstream and upstream connections, hop-by-hop versus end-to-end fields, termination points, authority, scheme, client address, and failure ownership.
misconceptions: A reverse proxy makes one transparent end-to-end HTTP connection from client to origin.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace downstream and upstream connections, hop-by-hop versus end-to-end fields, termination points, authority, scheme, client address, and failure ownership.

competencies.statement: Use ReverseProxy.Rewrite with separate inbound and outbound requests, SetURL and reviewed SetXForwarded behavior, and reject deprecated Director trust assumptions.
misconceptions: Director is equivalent to Rewrite and remains safe when it adds an authentication header.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ReverseProxy.Rewrite with separate inbound and outbound requests, SetURL and reviewed SetXForwarded behavior, and reject deprecated Director trust assumptions.

competencies.statement: Strip untrusted forwarding fields, rebuild them only at trusted hops, constrain backend targets, remove hop-by-hop state, and prevent header spoofing.
misconceptions: Appending a trusted proxy value to client-supplied X-Forwarded-For makes the entire chain trustworthy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Strip untrusted forwarding fields, rebuild them only at trusted hops, constrain backend targets, remove hop-by-hop state, and prevent header spoofing.

competencies.statement: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.
misconceptions: Because Go parses the front-end request safely, every downstream proxy and application will interpret it identically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.

competencies.statement: Handle unparsable query parameters, backend timeouts, bounded bodies, ModifyResponse, ErrorHandler, streaming, Alt-Svc, and sanitized failure evidence.
misconceptions: Copying RawQuery always preserves intent and cannot create a parser differential with the backend.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle unparsable query parameters, backend timeouts, bounded bodies, ModifyResponse, ErrorHandler, streaming, Alt-Svc, and sanitized failure evidence.

competencies.statement: Use httptest.NewRequestWithContext, NewRecorder, Result, headers, body limits, route values, and table cases while naming recorder limitations.
misconceptions: ResponseRecorder.Code always contains the implicit 200 even when a handler writes nothing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use httptest.NewRequestWithContext, NewRecorder, Result, headers, body limits, route values, and table cases while naming recorder limitations.

competencies.statement: Use NewServer or NewTLSServer for routing, middleware, redirects, cookies, protocol, streaming, and client-server integration with deterministic cleanup.
misconceptions: A ResponseRecorder test proves listener, TLS, connection, flush, and full middleware behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use NewServer or NewTLSServer for routing, middleware, redirects, cookies, protocol, streaming, and client-server integration with deterministic cleanup.

competencies.statement: Test accepted, invalid, absent, unauthorized, forbidden, stale, duplicate, oversized, canceled, timed-out, dependency-failed, and changed-schema cases.
misconceptions: High line coverage proves the handler contract and error mapping are correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test accepted, invalid, absent, unauthorized, forbidden, stale, duplicate, oversized, canceled, timed-out, dependency-failed, and changed-schema cases.

competencies.statement: Fuzz bounded request targets, headers, JSON, cursors, signatures, and policy inputs with invariants, seed corpus, reproducibility, and no live authority.
misconceptions: A fuzz test is useful only if it discovers a crash rather than a semantic invariant violation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fuzz bounded request targets, headers, JSON, cursors, signatures, and policy inputs with invariants, seed corpus, reproducibility, and no live authority.

competencies.statement: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.
misconceptions: Passing go test -race proves production has no races, deadlocks, leaks, or overload failures.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.

competencies.statement: Emit one structured request completion record with request and trace IDs, method, route pattern, status, duration, bytes, trusted peer class, and redaction.
misconceptions: Logging the raw URL and all request headers is the most useful production diagnostic record.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Emit one structured request completion record with request and trace IDs, method, route pattern, status, duration, bytes, trusted peer class, and redaction.

competencies.statement: Measure duration distributions, active requests, request and response bytes, status classes, cancellations, rejects, saturation, and dependencies with bounded attributes.
misconceptions: Using resource ID as a metric label makes endpoint latency easier to diagnose safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure duration distributions, active requests, request and response bytes, status classes, cancellations, rejects, saturation, and dependencies with bounded attributes.

competencies.statement: Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.
misconceptions: Every HTTP 4xx response should set a server span status of Error.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.

competencies.statement: Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency, drain, and deep diagnostics; bound their cost; and prevent sensitive disclosure.
misconceptions: Liveness should fail whenever a downstream database is briefly unavailable so the process restarts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency, drain, and deep diagnostics; bound their cost; and prevent sensitive disclosure.

competencies.statement: Use benchmarks, pprof in a protected plane, traces, saturation, tail latency, allocation, contention, load shape, and changed-fault evidence for capacity and incidents.
misconceptions: A lower microbenchmark nanoseconds-per-operation proves the deployed server can handle more user traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use benchmarks, pprof in a protected plane, traces, saturation, tail latency, allocation, contention, load shape, and changed-fault evidence for capacity and incidents.

competencies.statement: Describe OpenAPI 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits.
misconceptions: Generated OpenAPI is automatically complete and correct when every route appears once.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe OpenAPI 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits.

competencies.statement: Compare specification, route registration, transport types, validators, examples, tests, auth policy, and observed responses to detect bidirectional drift.
misconceptions: Schema validation alone proves handler semantics, authorization, and side effects match documentation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare specification, route registration, transport types, validators, examples, tests, auth policy, and observed responses to detect bidirectional drift.

competencies.statement: Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, status, errors, ordering, auth, caching, and behavior; then plan deprecation and migration.
misconceptions: Adding an optional response field is always non-breaking for every client and signature scheme.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, status, errors, ordering, auth, caching, and behavior; then plan deprecation and migration.

competencies.statement: Build once, verify artifact and config, expand storage safely, stage readiness, canary, observe, drain with Shutdown, contract storage, and rollback by invariant.
misconceptions: If the new binary starts and health is green, schema contraction and full traffic are safe immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build once, verify artifact and config, expand storage safely, stage readiness, canary, observe, drain with Shutdown, contract storage, and rollback by invariant.

competencies.statement: Defend outcomes, semantics, routes, architecture, limits, persistence, identity, policy, browser and proxy trust, tests, observability, capacity, documentation, release, recovery, and residual risk.
misconceptions: A green deployment and passing smoke request complete production readiness and eliminate the need for rollback drills.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend outcomes, semantics, routes, architecture, limits, persistence, identity, policy, browser and proxy trust, tests, observability, capacity, documentation, release, recovery, and residual risk.

credential.title: HTTP Servers in Go 1.26: Secure Production APIs Certificate
